conceptualQuestions.prompt: What is the difference between @NotNull, @NotEmpty, and @NotBlank?
options: @NotNull: field is not null; @NotEmpty: not null AND not empty string/collection; @NotBlank: not null, not empty, and not all whitespace — use @NotBlank for String text fields
conceptualQuestions.correctAnswer: @NotNull: field is not null; @NotEmpty: not null AND not empty string/collection; @NotBlank: not null, not empty, and not all whitespace — use @NotBlank for String text fields
conceptualQuestions.explanation: For a String ' ' (spaces only): @NotNull passes (not null), @NotEmpty passes (not empty), @NotBlank FAILS (all whitespace). Use @NotBlank for user-input text. Use @NotEmpty for collections/arrays. Use @NotNull for any object reference.

conceptualQuestions.prompt: What does `@Valid` do when placed before a @RequestBody parameter?
options: Triggers Bean Validation on the deserialized object — processes all constraint annotations (@NotBlank, @Email, etc.) and throws MethodArgumentNotValidException if any fail
conceptualQuestions.correctAnswer: Triggers Bean Validation on the deserialized object — processes all constraint annotations (@NotBlank, @Email, etc.) and throws MethodArgumentNotValidException if any fail
conceptualQuestions.explanation: @Valid activates the validation framework. Jackson deserializes the JSON body first, then validation runs. If any @NotBlank, @Size, @Email etc. fail, Spring throws MethodArgumentNotValidException with all field errors before the controller method body executes.

conceptualQuestions.prompt: What are the three required elements in a custom constraint annotation?
options: message(), groups(), payload() — all three must be present in every constraint annotation; otherwise the annotation is not a valid constraint
conceptualQuestions.correctAnswer: message(), groups(), payload() — all three must be present in every constraint annotation; otherwise the annotation is not a valid constraint
conceptualQuestions.explanation: Bean Validation spec requires: String message() default '...'; Class<?>[] groups() default {}; Class<? extends Payload>[] payload() default {};. Plus @Constraint(validatedBy=...) to link to the validator. Without these three, the annotation is not a valid constraint.

conceptualQuestions.prompt: What class do you implement to write a custom constraint validator?
options: ConstraintValidator<A extends Annotation, T> — A is the annotation type, T is the type being validated; implement isValid(T value, ConstraintValidatorContext ctx)
conceptualQuestions.correctAnswer: ConstraintValidator<A extends Annotation, T> — A is the annotation type, T is the validator type; implement isValid(T value, ConstraintValidatorContext ctx)
conceptualQuestions.explanation: ConstraintValidator<UniqueEmail, String>: A=UniqueEmail (the annotation), T=String (the annotated field type). isValid() returns true if the value is valid. ConstraintValidatorContext allows custom error messages. Can be a Spring @Component to inject repositories/services.

conceptualQuestions.prompt: How do you validate that a list's elements pass a constraint (e.g., each tag in List<String> is @NotBlank)?
options: Declare the annotation on the type parameter: List<@NotBlank @Size(max=30) String> tags — Bean Validation processes each element
Spring validates collections automatically without annotations
conceptualQuestions.correctAnswer: Declare the annotation on the type parameter: List<@NotBlank @Size(max=30) String> tags — Bean Validation processes each element
conceptualQuestions.explanation: Jakarta Bean Validation 2.0+ supports container element validation. List<@NotBlank String> tags applies @NotBlank to each element in the list. This also works for Map values: Map<String, @NotNull BigDecimal> prices.

conceptualQuestions.prompt: What exception does @Validated method-level validation throw (as opposed to @RequestBody @Valid)?
options: ConstraintViolationException — thrown by the Bean Validation framework directly when @Validated method parameter constraints fail
conceptualQuestions.correctAnswer: ConstraintViolationException — thrown by the Bean Validation framework directly when @Validated method parameter constraints fail
conceptualQuestions.explanation: @RequestBody @Valid: Spring MVC catches the validation failure and wraps it in MethodArgumentNotValidException. @Validated on the class with @Valid on method params: the AOP interceptor throws ConstraintViolationException directly. Both need handlers in @RestControllerAdvice.

conceptualQuestions.prompt: What is a class-level constraint validator used for?
options: Validating relationships between multiple fields on the same object — e.g., end date must be after start date
conceptualQuestions.correctAnswer: Validating relationships between multiple fields on the same object — e.g., end date must be after start date
conceptualQuestions.explanation: Field-level validators see only one field. Class-level validators (@Target(TYPE)) receive the whole object, enabling cross-field validation: startDate < endDate, password == confirmPassword, discount < basePrice. The @Constraint annotation is placed on the class declaration.

conceptualQuestions.prompt: What are validation groups and when are they useful?
options: Groups of validators that run in parallel
Marker interfaces that allow different validation rules for the same class in different contexts — e.g., create requires @NotNull id, update requires @Null id
Groups are deprecated in Jakarta Validation 3
conceptualQuestions.correctAnswer: Marker interfaces that allow different validation rules for the same class in different contexts — e.g., create requires @NotNull id, update requires @Null id
conceptualQuestions.explanation: @NotNull(groups=OnCreate.class) means: only validate this constraint when the OnCreate group is active. @Validated(OnCreate.class) activates only OnCreate group constraints. Different REST operations (POST vs PUT) activate different groups on the same DTO class.

conceptualQuestions.prompt: A custom @UniqueEmail validator needs to query the database. How can it access a Spring repository?
options: Implement the validator as a Spring @Component — Spring injects it and dependency injection is available
Custom validators cannot access Spring beans
conceptualQuestions.correctAnswer: Implement the validator as a Spring @Component — Spring injects it and dependency injection is available
conceptualQuestions.explanation: When Spring detects a @Component that implements ConstraintValidator, it manages it as a Spring bean. Constructor injection, @Autowired, @Value all work. Spring registers it with the Validator — no manual wiring needed.

conceptualQuestions.prompt: What is the correct error response structure for a validation failure?
options: Return 400 Bad Request with a map of {fieldName: errorMessage} pairs so the client knows which specific fields failed and why
Return 400 with the full exception message
conceptualQuestions.correctAnswer: Return 400 Bad Request with a map of {fieldName: errorMessage} pairs so the client knows which specific fields failed and why
conceptualQuestions.explanation: Good validation errors are actionable: {fieldErrors: {email: 'Invalid email format', name: 'must not be blank'}}. The client (Angular form) can highlight each invalid field. A generic 'Bad request' message forces the client to guess which fields to fix.

writtenConceptQuestions: Show a complete CreateOrderRequest record with 6 different constraint annotations. Include nested validation on a List<OrderItemRequest>.
Explain the difference between @NotNull, @NotEmpty, and @NotBlank. For which Java types is each appropriate?
Write a custom @UniqueEmail constraint: the annotation and the ConstraintValidator implementation that queries a UserRepository.
Show the @ExceptionHandler for both MethodArgumentNotValidException (from @RequestBody @Valid) and ConstraintViolationException (from @Validated method params).
What are class-level constraints? Write a @ValidDateRange constraint that validates endDate is after startDate on a DateRangeRequest.
Explain validation groups with OnCreate and OnUpdate interfaces. Show how @Validated(OnCreate.class) activates only create-specific rules.
What does `List<@NotBlank @Size(max=30) String> tags` validate? How does container element validation work in Jakarta Bean Validation 2?
businessScenarios: A user registration form submits an email that's already taken. The current API returns 500 (DB unique constraint). Add a @UniqueEmail custom validator that returns 400 with message 'Email already registered' before hitting the DB.
A promotion creation API has startDate and endDate fields. Users submit endDate before startDate and get a 500 from business logic. Add a class-level @ValidDateRange constraint.

An order API validation returns 400 but the error body is just 'Bad Request'. Angular form doesn't know which fields to highlight. Fix the @ExceptionHandler to return {fieldErrors: {fieldName: message}} structure.

dsaPractice.title: Find Maximum in Array
dsaPractice.statement: Given an integer array nums, return the largest value. If nums is empty, return Integer.MIN_VALUE.
hints: Keep one variable for best value seen so far.

dsaPractice.title: Count Target Occurrences
dsaPractice.statement: Given an integer array nums and an integer target, return how many times target appears.
hints: Increment count when nums[i] equals target.

dsaPractice.title: API Data Transformations Practice
dsaPractice.statement: Solve a LeetCode-style problem using today's DSA focus. Clearly state input, output, edge cases, and complexity before coding.
examples: Use at least two custom test cases
hints: Start with brute force if stuck.
Then ask whether a map, two pointers, or sliding window improves it.

codingTasks.title: Implement Validation in Spring Boot

examples: Request DTO and Response DTO example
constraints: Follow layered architecture: Controller -> Service -> Repository
testCases: Invalid request returns 400 with error message
approach: Read input/output types from the method signature before writing a single line
Walk through the example by hand — trace each step manually
Write the simplest correct solution first (brute force is fine to start)
Handle edge cases: empty array/string, null, zero, negative numbers, single element
Verify your return type matches the signature (int vs long vs String vs int[])
Run through the test cases mentally before submitting

codingTasks.statement: Write a service method createOrder(CreateOrderRequest req) that: validates the request (non-null items, positive amounts), checks stock availability, saves the order, and returns an OrderResponse DTO. Throw custom exceptions for validation failures.
examples: Valid request -> OrderResponse with NEW status
testCases: valid input -> OrderResponse with correct fields
approach: Read input/output types from the method signature before writing a single line
Walk through the example by hand — trace each step manually
Write the simplest correct solution first (brute force is fine to start)
Handle edge cases: empty array/string, null, zero, negative numbers, single element
Verify your return type matches the signature (int vs long vs String vs int[])

codingTasks.title: Complex SQL for business reporting
codingTasks.statement: Write a SQL query to generate a monthly sales report: sum of total_amount by month and year, number of orders, and average order value. Show only months with more than 10 orders.
constraints: Use DATE_FORMAT or EXTRACT for month/year
codingTasks.starterCode:
-- Monthly sales report
SELECT 
    YEAR(order_date) as year,
    MONTH(order_date) as month,
    COUNT(*) as order_count,
    SUM(total_amount) as total_sales,
    AVG(total_amount) as avg_order
FROM orders
GROUP BY YEAR(order_date), MONTH(order_date)
HAVING COUNT(*) > 10
ORDER BY year, month;
approach: Read input/output types from the method signature before writing a single line
Walk through the example by hand — trace each step manually
Write the simplest correct solution first (brute force is fine to start)
Handle edge cases: empty array/string, null, zero, negative numbers, single element
Verify your return type matches the signature (int vs long vs String vs int[])
Run through the test cases mentally before submitting

codingTasks.title: Diameter of Binary Tree
codingTasks.statement: Given the root of a binary tree, return the length of the diameter. The diameter is the length of the longest path between any two nodes. This path may or may not pass through the root.
approach: State the problem clearly: what is the input, what is the output?
Brute force first — even O(n²) is fine as a starting point
Ask: what data structure reduces the work? HashMap for O(1) lookup, Set to track seen values
Handle edge cases: empty array, single element, all same values, negative numbers
Trace through one example step by step with your chosen algorithm
State time complexity (O(n), O(n log n)) and space complexity (O(1), O(n))

sqlPractice: SQL: Write a SELECT query for the CRUD SQL topic using a realistic table such as customers, orders, employees, or products.
SQL: Write a practical query that a business user could use for reporting, filtering, or auditing.
springBootScenarios: Spring Boot: Explain Spring Boot Core and where it fits in controller-service-repository architecture.
Spring Boot: Design one API endpoint that uses today's Java topic and returns a clean DTO.
Spring Boot: List validations and error responses needed for the business workflow.
angularQuestions: Angular: Explain HTTP Integration in simple words.
Angular: Design UI states for loading, success, empty data, and backend error.
Angular: What TypeScript model/interface would you create for today's API response?
writtenModelAnswers: Spring Boot Validation uses javax.validation annotations on DTO fields: @NotNull (not null), @NotBlank (not null, not whitespace), @Size(min, max), @Email, @Min, @Max, @Pattern. Add @Valid to the @RequestBody parameter in the controller to trigger validation. On failure, Spring throws MethodArgumentNotValidException.

Validation is the first line of defense in the API layer. In a Spring Boot order service, the CreateOrderRequest is validated (@NotNull orderId, @Min(1) quantity) before reaching the service. A @ControllerAdvice handles MethodArgumentNotValidException and returns a 400 Bad Request with field-level error messages.

Skipping input validation lets invalid data reach the service and database, causing constraint violations, data corruption, or security issues. Validating inside the service instead of in the DTO couples validation logic to business logic and makes it hard to test. Not returning field-level error messages in the 400 response makes debugging difficult for the frontend.

In an order or customer workflow, misusing this concept can cause incorrect totals, wrong status updates, or missing customer records — directly harming business trust. Understanding it correctly ensures data integrity and reliable workflows that users depend on.

Spring Boot relies on Java as its foundation, so this concept appears in service beans, entity classes, and utility methods. Knowing it well prevents subtle bugs in backend logic that are hard to debug when an API returns wrong data.

Angular should show a spinner or disabled button while the request is in progress, display the data clearly on success, and show a user-friendly message with a retry option on error. Never leave the user staring at a blank screen — always communicate the state.

A useful SQL pattern for this day is: SELECT customer_id, COUNT(*) as total FROM orders WHERE status = 'active' GROUP BY customer_id ORDER BY total DESC. This shows which customers have the most active orders, helping the business prioritize support and retention.

Always test null/empty input, a single-element collection or string, boundary values (min and max), duplicate values, negative numbers where applicable, and large inputs near the constraint limit. Edge cases are where most production bugs hide.

In an interview I would say: state the data structure or algorithm, explain why it is the best fit for this problem, walk through one example manually before coding, write clean code with meaningful names, and state time and space complexity at the end.

Five-point summary: (1) define the concept clearly, (2) explain why Java or the framework needs it, (3) describe how it works internally step by step, (4) give a real full-stack project usage example, (5) name the most common mistake and how to avoid it.
businessModelAnswers: Ask: What fields are required vs optional? What are the size or format constraints for each field? What should the error message say for each validation failure? Should custom validation logic be added with a @Constraint annotation? How will the frontend display the field-level errors?

Check the server logs first to find which request triggered the wrong data. Then query the database directly to compare expected vs actual values. Add structured logging to the relevant service method (log inputs, outputs, and any validation failures) so future issues can be diagnosed in minutes, not hours.

The API response would be: { "metric": "active_orders", "value": 142, "period": "2024-06-09" }. The service runs: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM orders WHERE status='active' AND DATE(created_at)=CURDATE(). This gives the manager a daily health indicator without needing database access.
